application: Allegro Modernisation PoC — Java Swing WebSocket Bridge

rules.rule_name: Multi-Field OR Search Logic
rules.description: A customer record is included in search results if ANY of the provided search fields matches — the search uses OR logic, not AND. A user entering both a name and a ZIP will get results that match either criterion.
rules.conditions: Triggered when the user clicks the 'Suchen' (Search) button
rules.actions: Iterates the in-memory search_space array; pushes any record satisfying at least one non-empty field match into search_result
rules.exceptions: Fields left empty are automatically excluded from matching (see BR-004)

rules.rule_name: ZIP Code Exact Match Constraint
rules.description: The PLZ (Postleitzahl / ZIP code) field uses strict equality matching (==) rather than substring matching. A user must enter the full, exact ZIP code to retrieve a match on that field.
rules.conditions: When the ZIP input field is non-empty during a search
rules.actions: Compares entered ZIP value with element.zip using == (exact equality)
rules.exceptions: No partial or prefix ZIP matching is supported

rules.rule_name: Case-Insensitive Substring Matching for Text Fields
rules.description: All text search fields except ZIP (name/Nachname, first/Vorname, ort/Ort, street/Strasse, hausnr/Hausnummer) use case-insensitive substring matching. A search for 'may' will match 'Mayer'.
rules.conditions: When any text field other than ZIP is non-empty during a search
rules.actions: Converts both the search term and the field value to lowercase before calling indexOf()
rules.exceptions: ZIP code uses exact match (see BR-002)

rules.rule_name: Empty Search Fields Are Excluded from Matching
rules.description: Only form fields that have been filled in by the user participate in the search. An empty field does not produce matches (it does not match all records). The truthy check `this.formdata.last &&` gates the comparison.
rules.conditions: Always applied during searchPerson()
rules.actions: Field comparison is short-circuit evaluated — if formdata field is falsy, the OR clause for that field is skipped
rules.exceptions: None

rules.rule_name: Fixed In-Memory Customer Dataset (Mock Data)
rules.description: The application operates on a hardcoded in-memory dataset of exactly 5 German customer records. This is a PoC mock; no database or backend API is queried. All data is embedded in Search.vue.
rules.conditions: Always active — there is no external data source
rules.actions: search_space array is used as the sole source of truth for all customer lookups
rules.exceptions: Any production implementation must replace this with a real API call

rules.rule_name: Zahlungsempfänger Must Be Selected Before Allegro Transfer
rules.description: Before transferring customer data to Allegro via WebSocket, the user must select one Zahlungsempfänger (payment recipient) from the secondary results table. If no selection is made, zahlungsempfaenger_selected defaults to an empty string and that empty value is sent.
rules.conditions: When the 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' button is clicked
rules.actions: The zahlungsempfaenger property of the outgoing JSON is replaced with the single selected zahlungsempfaenger object (or empty string if none selected)
rules.exceptions: No hard enforcement — if no selection is made, an empty string is sent in the zahlungsempfaenger field without error

rules.rule_name: Zahlungsempfänger Array Replaced with Single Selection on Transfer
rules.description: When preparing the customer record for transfer to Allegro, the full array of Zahlungsempfänger records is replaced with only the single user-selected payment recipient. The outgoing payload carries exactly one payment record.
rules.conditions: Only when target == 'textfield' (Allegro transfer, not textarea sync)
rules.actions: Deep-clones the selected_result object, then overwrites zahlungsempfaenger with the single selected entry
rules.exceptions: Textarea sync (target == 'textarea') does not apply this substitution

rules.rule_name: Each Customer May Have One to Three Zahlungsempfänger
rules.description: In the mock dataset, each customer record contains an array of 1 to 3 Zahlungsempfänger entries. The system supports any number of payment recipients per customer, with the user selecting which one to transfer.
rules.conditions: Structural property of the customer data model
rules.actions: The Zahlungsempfänger table renders all entries for the selected customer, allowing row selection
rules.exceptions: No upper bound is enforced beyond the mock data

rules.rule_name: Zahlungsempfänger Record Contains IBAN, BIC, and Valid-From Date
rules.description: Each Zahlungsempfänger (payment recipient) record must contain three core fields: IBAN (bank account number), BIC (bank identifier code), and valid_from (the date from which this payment record is valid). The fields valid_until and type are present in the mock data but always empty.
rules.conditions: Required structure for any Zahlungsempfänger used in the Allegro transfer
rules.actions: These three fields are transmitted to Allegro and populated into the IBAN, BIC, and 'Gültig ab' form fields
rules.exceptions: valid_until and type are tracked in the data model but currently always empty (not transferred to Allegro)

rules.rule_name: WebSocket Message Routing by Target Field
rules.description: Every WebSocket message carries a 'target' field that routes it to the correct UI component. The value 'textfield' triggers population of all customer data fields in the Swing form; the value 'textarea' updates only the freeform text area.
rules.conditions: Applied on every incoming WebSocket message in the Java Swing client
rules.actions: Switch on message.target: 'textarea' → setText on textArea; 'textfield' → parse JSON and populate all text fields
rules.exceptions: Any unrecognised target value is silently ignored (no default case in the switch)

rules.rule_name: Complete Customer Record Transferred as Single Atomic Bundle
rules.description: When the user clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen', the entire customer record (all personal and selected payment fields) is serialised as a single JSON payload and sent over WebSocket in one message. There is no mechanism for partial or incremental field transfer.
rules.conditions: User clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' with a customer selected
rules.actions: Serialises person + selected Zahlungsempfänger into one JSON object and sends via WebSocket
rules.exceptions: None

rules.rule_name: Unconditional Broadcast to All Connected Clients
rules.description: The Node.js relay server re-broadcasts every received message to ALL currently connected clients without any filtering, targeting, or authentication. Both the originating Vue client and any connected Swing clients receive the same message.
rules.conditions: Triggered on every incoming WebSocket message
rules.actions: Iterates the clients array and calls sendUTF(json) on each
rules.exceptions: No sender exclusion — the originating client also receives its own message back

rules.rule_name: Origin-Agnostic WebSocket Connection Acceptance
rules.description: The Node.js server accepts WebSocket connections from any origin by passing null as the accepted origin in request.accept(). No origin validation, authentication, or authorisation is applied.
rules.conditions: Any client attempts to establish a WebSocket connection
rules.actions: Connection is accepted unconditionally with request.accept(null, request.origin)
rules.exceptions: Production deployment must implement origin whitelisting and authentication

rules.rule_name: Only UTF-8 Text Messages Are Relayed
rules.description: The Node.js relay server only processes and re-broadcasts messages of type 'utf8'. Binary frames or other message types are silently discarded.
rules.conditions: On every incoming WebSocket message frame
rules.actions: if (message.type === 'utf8') { broadcast } — non-UTF8 messages are ignored
rules.exceptions: None

rules.rule_name: Gender Is a Mutually Exclusive Single Selection
rules.description: In the Allegro Swing form, the three gender options (Weiblich/Female, Männlich/Male, Divers/Diverse) are grouped into a ButtonGroup, enforcing that at most one can be selected at any time. Selecting one automatically deselects the others.
rules.conditions: Any time a gender radio button is clicked in the Swing UI
rules.actions: Java ButtonGroup enforces single selection; the previously selected option is automatically deselected
rules.exceptions: The underlying model stores gender as three independent Boolean values (FEMALE/MALE/DIVERSE), creating a model-vs-UI inconsistency — see BR-026

rules.rule_name: Default Gender is Female (Weiblich) at Form Initialisation
rules.description: When the Allegro Swing form is first initialised and after every successful HTTP submission (form reset), the gender selection defaults to 'Weiblich' (Female). Male and Diverse are set to unselected.
rules.conditions: On initial form load and after every successful form submission
rules.actions: female.setSelected(true); male.setSelected(false); diverse.setSelected(false)
rules.exceptions: This default can be overridden by an incoming WebSocket 'textfield' message if the payload includes gender fields

rules.rule_name: Full Form Population from Incoming WebSocket Data
rules.description: When a 'textfield' WebSocket message is received, all matching form fields in the Swing client are overwritten with the values from the payload. The entire form is repopulated atomically from the incoming customer record.
rules.conditions: On receipt of a WebSocket message with target == 'textfield'
rules.actions: Parses the JSON payload into a SearchResult struct and calls setText() on each Swing text field
rules.exceptions: Fields present in the form but absent from the JSON payload (e.g., missing keys) are set to null/empty by default Java behaviour

rules.rule_name: House Number (Hausnummer) Present in Legacy Client but Absent from MVP Model
rules.description: The legacy monolithic Swing client (websocket/Main.java) includes a dedicated text field for Hausnummer (tf_hausnr) and parses it from the WebSocket JSON ('hausnr' key). However, the MVP model (ModelProperties enum) and the OpenAPI spec do NOT include a house number field. This is a field mapping gap between the two implementations.
rules.conditions: Structural — present whenever customer data is transferred
rules.actions: Legacy client populates tf_hausnr from 'hausnr' JSON key; MVP model silently loses the house number on HTTP submission
rules.exceptions: None — this is a gap requiring resolution

rules.rule_name: Post-Submission Form Reset to Default State
rules.description: After a successful HTTP POST submission (non-empty response from HTTPBin), all editable form fields in the Allegro Swing UI are cleared to empty strings, and the gender is reset to Female. The response body is displayed in the text area as confirmation.
rules.conditions: Triggered by the EventEmitter when PocModel.action() receives a non-empty HTTP response
rules.actions: Clears: firstName, name, dateOfBirth, zip, ort, street, iban, bic, validFrom; sets female=true, male=false, diverse=false; sets textArea to response body
rules.exceptions: The reset is NOT triggered on failure (empty response) — the form retains its data

rules.rule_name: All 13 Domain Fields Submitted Atomically in HTTP POST
rules.description: When the 'Anordnen' button is clicked, all 13 model properties are serialised and submitted together in a single HTTP POST request to the HTTPBin endpoint. There is no selective or partial submission. Fields with null values are submitted as the string 'null'.
rules.conditions: User clicks the 'Anordnen' button in the Swing form
rules.actions: Iterates all ModelProperties enum values, adds each to a HashMap, calls HttpBinService.post(data)
rules.exceptions: NULL RISK: model.get(val).getField().toString() will throw NullPointerException if any field value is null — all fields are initialised to null and only populated if the user types or receives WebSocket data

rules.rule_name: Non-Empty HTTP Response Body Indicates Success
rules.description: The Allegro Swing client determines whether the HTTP POST submission succeeded by checking whether the response body string is non-empty. An empty body is treated as a failure and emits 'Failed operation'. HTTP status codes are not evaluated.
rules.conditions: After HttpBinService.post() returns
rules.actions: if (!responseBody.isEmpty()) → emit response body (success path); else → emit 'Failed operation'
rules.exceptions: HTTP 4xx/5xx errors are not distinguished from success — only body emptiness is checked. A 500 response with a non-empty body would be treated as success.

rules.rule_name: HTTP POST Uses Fixed JSON Content-Type
rules.description: All HTTP POST submissions to the backend endpoint use Content-Type: application/json. The request body is a flat JSON object with all 13 field names as keys.
rules.conditions: Every HTTP submission
rules.actions: Sets Connection-Type header to 'application/json'; generates JSON using javax.json streaming API
rules.exceptions: None

rules.rule_name: HTTP Endpoint is Hardcoded to Localhost HTTPBin
rules.description: The HTTP submission target URL is hardcoded as http://localhost:8080/post pointing to a local HTTPBin Docker container. This is a PoC-only endpoint; production integration with the real Allegro backend requires parameterisation.
rules.conditions: Always — no configuration mechanism exists
rules.actions: All form submissions are sent to the hardcoded URL
rules.exceptions: Production deployment must externalise this URL to a configuration file or environment variable
rules.code_example:
public static final String URL = "http://localhost:8080";
public static final String PATH = "/post";

rules.rule_name: 13 Canonical Domain Fields Define the Customer Record
rules.description: The authoritative set of customer domain fields is defined by the ModelProperties enum and mirrored in the OpenAPI spec. The 13 fields are: TEXT_AREA, FIRST_NAME, LAST_NAME, DATE_OF_BIRTH, ZIP, ORT, STREET, IBAN, BIC, VALID_FROM, FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE.
rules.conditions: Structural — defines the complete data contract between Swing client and HTTPBin backend
rules.actions: All 13 fields are initialised in PocModel, bound to UI components in PocPresenter, and serialised in every HTTP POST
rules.exceptions: Hausnummer (house number) is present in the legacy client and Vue UI but absent from this canonical list — see BR-018

rules.rule_name: All Fields Initialised to Null — No Default Values
rules.description: Every model field is initialised with null as its initial value in PocModel. There are no default values for any customer data field. Fields remain null until the user types, a WebSocket message arrives, or a form reset occurs.
rules.conditions: At application startup and after re-initialisation
rules.actions: model.put(ModelProperties.FIRST_NAME, new ValueModel<String>(null)) for each field
rules.exceptions: Calling model.get(val).getField().toString() on any null field will cause NullPointerException — see BR-020
rules.code_example:
model.put(ModelProperties.FIRST_NAME, new ValueModel<String>(null));
model.put(ModelProperties.MALE, new ValueModel<Boolean>(null));

rules.rule_name: Gender Stored as Three Independent Booleans (Model Inconsistency Risk)
rules.description: Gender is represented in the domain model as three independent Boolean fields (FEMALE, MALE, DIVERSE) rather than a single enumerated value. This means the model can technically represent invalid states where two or all three are simultaneously true. The UI enforces mutual exclusivity via ButtonGroup, but the model does not.
rules.conditions: Structural — exists whenever gender data is stored or transmitted
rules.actions: Three separate ChangeListeners independently update FEMALE, MALE, and DIVERSE Boolean values in the model
rules.exceptions: The Swing ButtonGroup prevents simultaneous true values at the UI level, but programmatic model manipulation bypasses this guard

rules.rule_name: Date Fields Use ISO 8601 Format (YYYY-MM-DD)
rules.description: All date values in the system — Date of Birth (dob/DATE_OF_BIRTH) and Zahlungsempfänger Valid-From (valid_from/VALID_FROM) — are represented as ISO 8601 strings in YYYY-MM-DD format, as demonstrated by the mock dataset.
rules.conditions: Any time a date value is included in a customer record
rules.actions: Dates are treated as plain strings throughout the pipeline — no date parsing, validation, or formatting is applied
rules.exceptions: No format validation is enforced — non-ISO strings would be accepted silently

rules.rule_name: Kundennummer Present in Search and Display but Not Transmitted to Allegro API
rules.description: The customer number (knr / Kundennummer) is present in the mock dataset, displayed in the search results table, and included in the WebSocket payload sent to the Allegro Swing client. However, it is NOT defined in the ModelProperties enum and NOT included in the OpenAPI specification for the HTTP POST endpoint. It is therefore lost during the HTTP submission step.
rules.conditions: Occurs in every end-to-end transfer that includes the customer number
rules.actions: knr appears in search table display and WebSocket JSON payload, but has no corresponding field in the MVP model or API spec
rules.exceptions: None — this is a data loss gap

rules.rule_name: No Input Validation Exists Anywhere in the Pipeline
rules.description: Neither the Vue.js search form, the Node.js relay, nor the Java Swing client applies any input validation, type checking, format verification, or business constraint enforcement on user-entered data before submission. The absence of validation is a systemic gap.
rules.conditions: Always — no validation logic is present in the codebase
rules.actions: All values are accepted as-is; invalid IBANs, malformed dates, and empty required fields are passed through without error
rules.exceptions: The absence of validation IS the exception — production implementation must add comprehensive validation

rules.rule_name: Live Textarea Content Synchronised to Allegro on Every Keystroke
rules.description: A Vue watcher on the internal_content_textarea data property triggers sendMessage() on every character change in the freeform textarea. This sends the full current textarea content to all WebSocket subscribers (including the Allegro Swing client) in real time.
rules.conditions: Any time the textarea content changes (every keystroke, paste, delete)
rules.actions: Calls sendMessage(val, 'textarea') which sends { target: 'textarea', content: val } over WebSocket
rules.exceptions: WebSocket must be open; no debouncing or throttling is applied — high-frequency messages possible during rapid typing

rules.rule_name: Textarea Sync Routed Separately from Customer Data Transfer
rules.description: Freeform textarea content uses target='textarea' routing, which is handled distinctly from customer data (target='textfield'). In the Swing client, textarea messages update only the text area component without touching any personal or payment data fields.
rules.conditions: On receipt of a WebSocket message with target == 'textarea'
rules.actions: textArea.setText(message.content) — only the JTextArea component is updated; all data fields remain unchanged
rules.exceptions: None

workflows.workflow_name: Customer Search and Allegro Data Transfer
workflows.description: The primary business workflow: a user searches for a German customer in the web UI, selects the customer and a payment recipient, then transfers all data to the Allegro legacy Swing desktop client via WebSocket.
workflows.trigger: User interacts with the Vue.js Search UI

steps.name: Enter Search Criteria
steps.description: User fills in one or more fields in the search form (Name, Vorname, PLZ, Ort, Strasse, Hausnummer)

steps.name: Execute Search
steps.description: User clicks 'Suchen'. The system applies OR logic across all non-empty fields: text fields use case-insensitive substring match; PLZ uses exact equality match.

steps.name: Review Search Results
steps.description: Matching customer records are displayed in a results table showing Kdn.-nr, Name, Vorname, Geburtsdatum, PLZ, Strasse, Ort

steps.name: Select Customer Record
steps.description: User clicks a result row to select a customer. The selected row is highlighted. The customer's Zahlungsempfänger list is displayed in the secondary table.

steps.name: Select Zahlungsempfänger
steps.description: User selects one payment recipient row from the Zahlungsempfänger table (IBAN, BIC, Gültig ab). Only one may be selected.

steps.name: Initiate Allegro Transfer
steps.description: User clicks 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen'. The system clones the selected customer record, replaces the Zahlungsempfänger array with the single selected entry, and sends the payload as a JSON WebSocket message with target='textfield'.

steps.name: No Results Found
steps.description: No customer records matched the search criteria. The results table remains empty.

steps.name: Node.js Relay Broadcasts Message
steps.description: The Node.js server receives the UTF-8 JSON message and broadcasts it to all connected WebSocket clients without filtering.

steps.name: Allegro Swing Client Populates Form
steps.description: The Java Swing client receives the message, routes it via the 'textfield' target, parses the JSON payload, and sets all customer and payment fields in the desktop form.

steps.name: Transfer Complete
steps.description: All customer and payment data fields are now populated in the Allegro Swing form. The Allegro operator can review, edit, and submit.

end_states: Allegro form fully populated with customer and payment data
No search results found — user refines search criteria
WebSocket connection unavailable — data not transferred

workflows.description: The Allegro operator reviews the populated form, optionally edits fields, then clicks 'Anordnen' to submit all 13 domain fields via HTTP POST to the backend.
workflows.trigger: User clicks 'Anordnen' button in the Allegro Swing desktop form

steps.name: Review and Edit Form
steps.description: Allegro operator reviews all 13 form fields (personal data, gender, address, IBAN, BIC, valid_from, text area). Fields may be edited directly before submission.

steps.name: Click Anordnen
steps.description: User clicks the 'Anordnen' button. PocPresenter delegates to PocModel.action().

steps.name: Serialise All Fields to JSON
steps.description: All 13 ModelProperties are iterated. Each field value is converted to string and added to a HashMap. If any field is null, NullPointerException is thrown.

steps.name: HTTP POST to Backend
steps.description: HttpBinService sends the serialised JSON as HTTP POST to http://localhost:8080/post with Content-Type: application/json.

steps.name: Evaluate Response
steps.description: PocModel.action() checks whether the HTTP response body is non-empty. Note: HTTP status code is not checked.

steps.name: Success: Reset Form
steps.description: EventEmitter broadcasts the response body. PocPresenter clears all text fields, resets gender to Female (Weiblich), and displays the response in the text area.

steps.name: NullPointerException on Null Field
steps.description: If any model field is null (not yet populated), model.get(val).getField().toString() throws NPE. The RuntimeException propagates to the UI thread.

steps.name: Failure: Display Error
steps.description: If the response body is empty, EventEmitter emits 'Failed operation'. The text area displays this message; the form data is NOT cleared.

end_states: Success: form cleared, response body displayed in text area
Failure: 'Failed operation' shown in text area, form data retained
Error: NullPointerException if any field is null at submission time

workflows.description: A continuously active background workflow that synchronises the content of the Vue.js textarea to the Allegro Swing text area in real time on every keystroke.
workflows.trigger: User types, pastes, or deletes text in the Vue.js textarea

steps.name: User Modifies Textarea
steps.description: User types or pastes text in the Vue.js textarea component bound to internal_content_textarea

steps.name: Vue Watcher Triggers Send
steps.description: Vue's reactive watcher detects the value change and immediately calls sendMessage(val, 'textarea') — no debouncing applied

steps.name: WebSocket Message Sent
steps.description: JSON payload { target: 'textarea', content: <text> } is sent over WebSocket to Node.js relay

steps.name: Relay Broadcasts to All Clients
steps.description: Node.js broadcasts the message to all connected WebSocket clients

steps.name: Swing Client Updates Text Area
steps.description: Java Swing client routes the 'textarea' target message and calls textArea.setText(message.content)

end_states: Allegro text area reflects current Vue textarea content
Sync fails silently if WebSocket connection is closed

identified_gaps.title: No Input Validation Anywhere
identified_gaps.description: No validation of any kind (format, required fields, business constraints) exists in the entire pipeline
identified_gaps.recommendation: Implement IBAN checksum validation, ISO date format checks, required field enforcement, and minimum field length rules

identified_gaps.title: NullPointerException Risk on Form Submission
identified_gaps.description: PocModel.action() calls .toString() on potentially-null field values without null guards
identified_gaps.recommendation: Add null checks before .toString() or use String.valueOf(field) which handles null safely

identified_gaps.title: Hausnummer Field Gap Between Implementations
identified_gaps.description: House number exists in legacy client and Vue payload but is missing from ModelProperties enum and API spec
identified_gaps.recommendation: Add HAUS_NR to ModelProperties enum and to the OpenAPI PostObject schema

identified_gaps.title: Kundennummer Lost During HTTP Submission
identified_gaps.description: Customer number is present in search results and WebSocket payload but absent from the API spec and model
identified_gaps.recommendation: Add KNR (Kundennummer) to ModelProperties enum and API spec if business requires it at submission time

identified_gaps.title: No Zahlungsempfänger Selection Enforcement
identified_gaps.description: The system sends an empty string if the user clicks transfer without selecting a payment recipient
identified_gaps.recommendation: Disable or guard the 'Nach ALLEGRO übernehmen' button until a Zahlungsempfänger is selected

identified_gaps.title: No WebSocket Reconnection Logic
identified_gaps.description: Neither Java nor Vue clients attempt reconnection on disconnect, breaking the integration silently
identified_gaps.recommendation: Implement exponential back-off reconnection in both clients

identified_gaps.title: HTTP Status Code Not Evaluated
identified_gaps.description: Success is determined by response body emptiness, not HTTP status code — a 500 error with body is treated as success
identified_gaps.recommendation: Check connection.getResponseCode() before evaluating the body; treat 2xx as success only
